prompt:
あなたはTOEIC Part 6のテスト作成専門家です。
長文穴埋め問題を作成してください。

TOEIC Part 6の形式：
- 1つの文書（メール、記事、広告など）に4つの空欄
- 各空欄に4つの選択肢（A, B, C, D）
- 語彙・文法・文脈理解を総合的に評価
- ビジネス文書形式

重要な要求事項：
1. 自然で実用的なビジネス文書を作成
2. 文法的に正確で、ネイティブスピーカーが自然に感じる表現
3. TOEIC Part 6の形式と難易度に準拠
4. 4つの空欄を適切な位置に配置
5. 各空欄に4つの選択肢を提供
6. 正解は1つのみ、他は明確に不正解
7. 日本語解説も含める

出力形式はJSON形式で以下の構造にしてください：
{
  "documentType": "指定された文書タイプ",
  "title": "文書のタイトル",
  "fullText": "空欄を(1)、(2)、(3)、(4)で示した完全な文書",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": ["選択肢A", "選択肢B", "選択肢C", "選択肢D"],
      "correct": "A|B|C|D",
      "explanation": "なぜその答えが正しいかの説明（日本語）",
      "questionType": "vocabulary|grammar|context|sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "easy|medium|hard",
  "topic": "ビジネス分野",
  "wordCount": 文書の単語数
}

以下の条件でTOEIC Part 6問題を1問作成してください：

【生成条件】
- 難易度: hard
- 文書タイプ: notice (Notice/Announcement)
- ビジネストピック: office_announcements (オフィス告知)
- 空欄数: 4個

【特別な要求】
各空欄の正解位置は以下のように配置してください：
- 空欄1の正解位置: C
- 空欄2の正解位置: D
- 空欄3の正解位置: A
- 空欄4の正解位置: B

【問題タイプの配分】
- 空欄1: context (文脈理解)
- 空欄2: sentence_insertion (文挿入)
- 空欄3: grammar (文法・語形)
- 空欄4: vocabulary (語彙選択)

JSON形式で問題を作成してください。

{
  "documentType": "notice (Notice/Announcement)",
  "title": "Upcoming Office Renovation and Temporary Workspace Reassignment",
  "fullText": "To: All staff\nFrom: Facilities Management\nDate: August 14\n\nSubject: Upcoming Office Renovation and Temporary Workspace Reassignment\n\nAs part of our ongoing effort to modernize the office environment, the 3rd-floor open-plan area will undergo extensive renovations beginning Monday, August 24 and continuing through November. (1) During this period, affected employees will be reassigned to temporary workspaces on the 5th floor and in the annex building. The Facilities team recognizes that this transition may cause disruption to regular workflows; therefore, we have implemented measures to minimize inconvenience, such as staggered seating arrangements, additional booking slots for meeting rooms, and enhanced IT support. (2) We ask that department heads coordinate seating plans with HR by Friday, August 21 to ensure a smooth move. To facilitate daily operations, each reassigned workstation will be equipped with a docking station and a noise-cancellation headset. (3) Employees who require ergonomic chairs for medical reasons should submit a request to facilities@company.com before August 20. Please note that the company will not cover personal office supplies lost during the move. (4) If you have questions about specific accommodations or need assistance with packing, please contact the Facilities Helpdesk.",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": [
        "All meetings will be canceled until the renovation is complete.",
        "Parking spaces will be reassigned to visitors only.",
        "Noise and intermittent access restrictions should be expected.",
        "Remote work will be mandatory for all staff on the 3rd floor."
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "（日本語）正解はC。前後の文脈は工事による影響（作業の期間や一時移転）について述べているため、『騒音や断続的な出入り制限が予想される』という表現が自然に続きます。Aはすべての会議を中止するという極端な主張で文脈と合わず、Bは駐車場に関する別の話題で無関係、Dは在宅勤務を義務化するという重大な方針変更で本文のトーンや後続の移転対応（臨時ワークスペースの手配）と矛盾します。",
      "questionType": "context"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank2",
      "position": 2,
      "options": [
        "Employees are responsible for transporting their own desks and monitors.",
        "The cafeteria will be closed during weekends for maintenance.",
        "Please make sure to remove all personal items from communal areas before Friday.",
        "Facilities staff will be on-site throughout the relocation to provide packing materials and technical assistance."
      ],
      "correct": "D",
      "explanation": "（日本語）正解はD。前の文では『不便を最小限にするための対策』が列挙されており、その直後に『施設担当が移転期間中に現場で資材や技術支援を提供する』という文が続くことで、対策の具体例として自然につながります。Aは社員負担を示し本文の『対策を講じる』という趣旨と矛盾、Bは無関係な話題、Cは有用だが配置の上では部門長に座席計画の調整を依頼する流れの前に置くとやや唐突で、全体の論理構成上Dが最も適切です。",
      "questionType": "sentence_insertion"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank3",
      "position": 3,
      "options": [
        "submit",
        "submitting",
        "to submit",
        "have submitted"
      ],
      "correct": "A",
      "explanation": "（日本語）正解はA。助動詞shouldの後には動詞の原形が続くため 'should submit' が文法的に正しいです。Bの'submitting'は進行形の-ing形で不可、Cの'to submit'はshouldの後に不定詞をそのまま置く形で不適、Dの'have submitted'は完了形で文脈（申請の締切を示す助言）には合いません。",
      "questionType": "grammar"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank4",
      "position": 4,
      "options": [
        "Regrettably,",
        "However,",
        "Therefore,",
        "Similarly,"
      ],
      "correct": "B",
      "explanation": "（日本語）正解はB。直前の文は『会社は個人の備品紛失を補償しない』という注意であり、次に『具体的な配慮や梱包の支援が必要なら連絡してほしい』と続けるので、対照を示す'However,'（しかしながら）が適切です。Aの'Regrettably,'は残念さを表すが接続として弱く、Cの'Therefore,'は因果関係（だから〜する）を示し文脈に合わない、Dの'Similarly,'は類似性を示すため不適です。",
      "questionType": "vocabulary"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "topic": "ビジネス分野",
  "wordCount": 190
}

あなたはTOEIC Part 6問題の翻訳専門家です。
文書全体と選択肢を適切に日本語に翻訳してください。

重要なルール：
1. 文書は自然で分かりやすい日本語に翻訳
2. 与えられた文書は既に完成した文章なので、そのまま翻訳
3. 選択肢は1-4語の簡潔な日本語に翻訳
4. 選択肢に説明や解説は絶対に不要
5. 文脈に応じた最も適切な意味のみ
6. ビジネス用語は適切な日本語表現を使用
7. 4つの空欄すべてに対する選択肢翻訳を提供すること

出力形式：
{
  "titleTranslation": "タイトルの翻訳",
  "contentTranslation": "文書全体の翻訳（空欄なしの完全な文章として）",
  "questionsTranslations": [
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    },
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    }
  ]
}

次のTOEIC Part 6問題を翻訳してください：

タイトル：Upcoming Office Renovation and Temporary Workspace Reassignment

文書全体（正解が埋められた完全な文章）：
To: All staff
From: Facilities Management
Date: August 14

Subject: Upcoming Office Renovation and Temporary Workspace Reassignment

As part of our ongoing effort to modernize the office environment, the 3rd-floor open-plan area will undergo extensive renovations beginning Monday, August 24 and continuing through November. Noise and intermittent access restrictions should be expected. During this period, affected employees will be reassigned to temporary workspaces on the 5th floor and in the annex building. The Facilities team recognizes that this transition may cause disruption to regular workflows; therefore, we have implemented measures to minimize inconvenience, such as staggered seating arrangements, additional booking slots for meeting rooms, and enhanced IT support. Facilities staff will be on-site throughout the relocation to provide packing materials and technical assistance. We ask that department heads coordinate seating plans with HR by Friday, August 21 to ensure a smooth move. To facilitate daily operations, each reassigned workstation will be equipped with a docking station and a noise-cancellation headset. submit Employees who require ergonomic chairs for medical reasons should submit a request to facilities@company.com before August 20. Please note that the company will not cover personal office supplies lost during the move. However, If you have questions about specific accommodations or need assistance with packing, please contact the Facilities Helpdesk.

選択肢：
空欄1: A. All meetings will be canceled until the renovation is complete., B. Parking spaces will be reassigned to visitors only., C. Noise and intermittent access restrictions should be expected., D. Remote work will be mandatory for all staff on the 3rd floor.
空欄2: A. Employees are responsible for transporting their own desks and monitors., B. The cafeteria will be closed during weekends for maintenance., C. Please make sure to remove all personal items from communal areas before Friday., D. Facilities staff will be on-site throughout the relocation to provide packing materials and technical assistance.
空欄3: A. submit, B. submitting, C. to submit, D. have submitted
空欄4: A. Regrettably,, B. However,, C. Therefore,, D. Similarly,

重要：
1. 上記の文書は既に空欄に正解が埋められた完全な文章です
2. この完全な文章をそのまま自然な日本語に翻訳してください
3. 翻訳には空欄を含めないでください（元の文書は既に完成しています）
4. 選択肢は簡潔に翻訳してください
5. questionsTranslationsは必ず4つの要素を含む配列で、各要素が4つの選択肢翻訳を含むこと

JSON形式で翻訳を出力してください。
